gloss.en: the fact of being able to do something

words.example: She has the ability to learn fast.

gloss.en: in or to a foreign country

words.example: They went abroad to study.

gloss.en: to say yes to something offered

words.example: He asked me to marry him and I accepted.

gloss.en: an unexpected event causing injury or damage

words.example: There was a car accident on the road.

gloss.en: to succeed in reaching a goal

words.example: He had finally achieved success.

gloss.en: from one side to the other

words.example: We walked across the street.

gloss.en: in fact; really

words.example: What did she actually say?

gloss.en: a word that describes a noun

words.example: "Happy" is an adjective.

gloss.en: fully grown or developed

words.example: This show is for an adult audience.

gloss.en: a thing that helps you do better

words.example: Speaking English is a big advantage.

gloss.en: an exciting or dangerous experience

words.example: The trip was a great adventure.

gloss.en: a notice telling people about a product

words.example: I saw an advertisement for new phones.

gloss.en: the activity of telling people about products

words.example: Cigarette advertising has been banned.

gloss.en: a suggestion about what to do

words.example: She gave me good advice.

gloss.en: later in time than something

words.example: We had tea after lunch.

gloss.en: opposing somebody or something

words.example: They voted against the plan.

gloss.en: a sound showing surprise or pleasure

words.example: Ah, there you are!

gloss.en: the gases around the earth that we breathe

words.example: Let's go out for some fresh air.

gloss.en: a passage between rows of seats or shelves

words.example: She walked down the aisle to her seat.

gloss.en: living; not dead

words.example: Is your mother still alive?

gloss.en: the whole number or amount

words.example: All the children are here.

gloss.en: to let somebody do something

words.example: His parents won't allow him to stay out late.

gloss.en: nearly; not quite

words.example: I like almost all of them.

gloss.en: without any other people

words.example: She lives alone in the city.

gloss.en: moving forward; following the length of

words.example: We walked along the river.

gloss.en: before now or before a certain time

words.example: No thanks, I've already eaten.

gloss.en: good enough; okay; fine

words.example: Don't worry, everything is alright.

gloss.en: despite the fact that

words.example: Although the sun was shining, it wasn't warm.

gloss.en: surrounded by; in the middle of

words.example: There is a house among the trees.

gloss.en: finding something funny; entertained

words.example: The children were amused by the clown.

gloss.en: the feeling of finding something funny

words.example: She smiled with amusement.

gloss.en: very old; from long ago

words.example: We visited an ancient temple.

gloss.en: in an angry way

words.example: He shouted angrily at the driver.

gloss.en: the joint between the foot and the leg

words.example: She hurt her ankle while running.

gloss.en: some amount, used in questions or negatives

words.example: Do you have any money?

gloss.en: any person at all

words.example: Is there anybody who can help me?

gloss.en: any longer; from now on

words.example: I don't live here anymore.

gloss.en: in any case; besides

words.example: It's too expensive, and anyway I don't like it.

gloss.en: in or to any place

words.example: I can't see it anywhere.

gloss.en: a set of rooms for living, on one floor

words.example: They live in a small apartment.

gloss.en: to say sorry for something

words.example: He called to apologise for being late.

gloss.en: a program you download to a phone or tablet

words.example: You first need to install the app.

gloss.en: to start to be seen

words.example: A rash appeared on his arm.

gloss.en: the way somebody or something looks

words.example: She cares about her appearance.

gloss.en: to make a formal request for something

words.example: I want to apply for the job.

gloss.en: the art of designing buildings

words.example: She wants to study architecture.

gloss.en: a part of a place or region

words.example: They live in a quiet area.

gloss.en: to speak angrily because you disagree

words.example: My brothers are always arguing.

gloss.en: an angry discussion where people disagree

words.example: They had an argument about money.

gloss.en: a comfortable chair with side supports

words.example: He sat in the soft armchair.

gloss.en: approximately; about

words.example: He arrived around five o'clock.

gloss.en: used when comparing two things

words.example: You're as tall as your father.

gloss.en: sleeping

words.example: The baby is asleep now.

gloss.en: a person who helps someone in their work

words.example: She works as a shop assistant.

gloss.en: a person who travels in space

words.example: The astronaut walked on the moon.

gloss.en: a person who competes in sports

words.example: She is a famous Olympic athlete.

gloss.en: the smallest part of an element

words.example: Water is made of tiny atoms.

gloss.en: an act of violence to hurt somebody

words.example: The attack happened last night.

gloss.en: the act of listening or watching carefully

words.example: Please pay attention to the teacher.

gloss.en: pleasant to look at

words.example: She is a very attractive woman.

gloss.en: people watching or listening to something

words.example: The audience clapped loudly.

gloss.en: a person who writes books

words.example: Who is your favourite author?

gloss.en: to keep something bad from happening

gloss.en: very bad or unpleasant

words.example: That's an awful colour.

gloss.en: a person's family, education and experience

gloss.en: without skill or care

gloss.en: a sport hitting a shuttlecock over a net

gloss.en: a platform outside an upper window

gloss.en: the business done by banks

gloss.en: a meal cooked outdoors over a fire

gloss.en: a person who cuts men's hair

gloss.en: developed from or using something

words.example: The movie is based on a true story.

gloss.en: a team game throwing a ball into a net

words.example: He plays basketball every weekend.

gloss.en: a group of things made at one time

words.example: She baked a batch of cookies.

gloss.en: a seed eaten as a vegetable

words.example: We had rice and beans for dinner.

gloss.en: meat from a cow

words.example: We had roast beef for lunch.

gloss.en: past participle of the verb "be"

words.example: I have been to London twice.

gloss.en: an alcoholic drink made from malt

words.example: He drank a bottle of beer.

gloss.en: earlier than something; in the past

gloss.en: a person just starting to learn something

words.example: This class is for beginners.

gloss.en: the time when something starts

words.example: We met at the beginning of the year.

gloss.en: the way a person acts

words.example: His behavior was very rude.

gloss.en: the way a person acts toward others

words.example: The teacher praised his good behaviour.

gloss.en: the part of the body with the stomach

words.example: The baby has a round belly.

gloss.en: to be in the right place; to be owned

gloss.en: a strip worn around the waist

words.example: He wore a leather belt.

gloss.en: of the most excellent quality

words.example: That's the best movie I've ever seen!

gloss.en: in the space separating two things

words.example: The ball is between the two boxes.

gloss.en: the time when a baby is born

words.example: The baby weighed three kilos at birth.

gloss.en: a small flat sweet baked cake

words.example: She ate a chocolate biscuit.

gloss.en: a small amount; rather

gloss.en: a dark board for writing with chalk

words.example: The teacher wrote on the blackboard.

gloss.en: empty, with nothing written on it

words.example: Sign your name in the blank space.

gloss.en: pale gold in colour

words.example: She had long blonde hair.

gloss.en: the red liquid in the body

gloss.en: a flower on a plant

words.example: The garden was full of pink blooms.

gloss.en: to heat a liquid until it bubbles

words.example: Please boil the water for tea.

words.example: I had a boiled egg for breakfast.

gloss.en: a hard part of the body's skeleton

gloss.en: printed pages fastened inside a cover

gloss.en: a shop that sells books

words.example: I bought a map at the bookshop.

gloss.en: tired and impatient with nothing to do

gloss.en: to take and use something, then return it

gloss.en: a person in charge of others at work

gloss.en: the lowest part of something

gloss.en: a deep round dish for food

gloss.en: underwear that supports a woman's chest

gloss.en: the organ in the head that controls thought

gloss.en: a sudden clever idea or quick thinking

gloss.en: the part used to slow or stop a vehicle

gloss.en: a light, gentle wind

gloss.en: a structure built over a road or river

gloss.en: full of light; shining strongly

words.example: The sun was very bright today.

gloss.en: very clever or impressive

words.example: What a brilliant idea!

gloss.en: damaged; no longer whole or working
